author: @BurntKittyForge-pl8ol
text: off topic somewhat but i had this daydream windmill I'd like to see , it's ducted wind and has counter rotating turbines , like a huge steam turbine generator has , basically all the wind used if it was geared right and enough fin wheels counterotating , the size changes as the pressure decreases...i like the turbine vertical so the steam gives the flywheel. turbine shaft lift and a steam bushing for friction and centering , it seems the dumbest to use a heat source and complain it needs an electric blower when it's own heat to a coil tube generating steam at the nozzle can spin turbine with the other end of the shaft an air fan attacjed and then ducted as a blower seems a most basic addition that so simple and rugged could be part of any fire pit ...oh i liked your biodiesel to run a small diesel power generator and then charge ev with that seems ideal for me because the ev could double as a portable power bank for conversion to household electric outlet use , recharges multi source potentially remotely where windmill/ solar farm  attracts less attention for examp,e ...

author: @handydandy8094
text: Robert, I just had an interesting thought about paper and mechanical energy and figured I'd see what you think..!  I had heard that it was difficult or even impossible to fold a piece of paper x number of times and in practice breaking down recycling there's a natural spring effect from folding paper to a point and allowing it to act as a spring mechanism.?  Also I wondered if you couldn't design it as a circuit too if it was impregnated with graphene or electrodes maybe like paper origami circuitry or just a simple spring that lasts a long time?  I love all of your videos and like you am fascinated by the seemingly simple paper!

replies.author: @bryankotsch
replies.text: ​@ThinkingandTinkering You're absolutely brilliant and have probably forgotten more than most people ever learn. Next time I'm across the pond, I'd like to get together for a pint or spot of tea. Your choice, I'll buy.

replies.author: @bryankotsch
replies.text: ​@justtinkering6713 Actually I don't. And I have nerve damage from breaking my neck and back in Iraq. I have to rest my arms on books to lessen the shaking in my hands, especially under magnification. Otherwise it looks like I'm building during an earthquake 😂😂.  I'm 6 feet tall and about 300 pounds, so tiny hands would make me look hysterical 😂.  I'm better at building firearms.

author: @CrudelyMade
text:
you could probably cut the Archimedes bit out of hdpe or a plastic that doesn't have 3d layer lines and it would be much more slick. ;-) 
a lot of friction on 3d prints comes from the layers lines rubbing against layer lines.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I think you are right there mate
